title: Vector Databases & Search
content:
# Interview Prep: Vector DBs

## Q1: How does Product Quantization (PQ) compress vector embedding datasets?

### Standard Answer
PQ splits a $d$-dimensional vector into $m$ sub-vectors of dimension $d/m$. It clusters each sub-space independently using K-Means to build a codebook. Each sub-vector is then replaced by its nearest centroid index. This compresses a 1024-byte float vector to a small sequence of 8-bit cluster indices, reducing memory usage by 90% or more.
